class_max: 1 per naturalist, 1-3 naturalists available per day
description: This program introduces students to parts of Ohlone culture. Students will learn about East Bay Native Peoples, their connection to the land and Local natural resources, and traditional skills. Using hands-on activities and time outdoors, students will explore specimens and replica items that Native People still use today.
class_max_1: 1 per naturalist, 1-3 naturalists available per day

description: Discover the culture and traditions of East Bay Ohlone Peoples through virtual exploration. Via video conferencing, students will investigate how Native Peoples have long connected with the land and its natural resources.  Students will explore with a naturalist specimens and replica items that Native People still use today.

class_max: 1 per naturalist, 1-3 naturalists available per day
description: Explore the wetlands of Coyote Hills and its inhabitants to learn ways to conserve natural resources. Students will learn the types of plants and animals in wetland habitats and how they have adapted to survive. Meet animal ambassadors, explore specimens, and get time outside in this adaptable, exploration-heavy, and sensory-engaging program.
class_max_1: 1 per naturalist, 1-3 naturalists available per day
